name: fp8 block-scale dense GEMM

op.signature: out[M,N] = (A_deq) @ (B_deq).T  with A per-token-group fp8 e4m3, B per-block fp8 e4m3

preconditions: block == 128 (DeepSeek block-scale convention); operands already quantized to fp8 e4m3

numerics.notes:
Parity target is dequant-then-fp32-matmul. Both backends consume identical fp8 operands and dequant identically, so fp32-dot divergence is accumulation ORDER only (empirically max_rel ~2e-5 on GPU vs torch CUDA matmul, well inside the fp32 rtol 1e-3).

ARCH-DEPENDENT TF32 (issue #86): the Triton portable path forces input_precision="ieee" on the fp32 tl.dot, and the reference wraps its matmul in no_tf32() (utils.numeric) — otherwise torch's CUDA allow_tf32=True default truncates BOTH to TF32 on NVIDIA (10-bit mantissa) while AMD has no TF32, making "correctness" arch-dependent (the GB10 sm121 card false-failed at rel 96.5; root cause was TF32, NOT the e4m3fn-vs-fnuz format mismatch the issue body guessed — the operands are already correct e4m3fn on NVIDIA).

bf16 rtol = 0.016 (CALIBRATED 2026-06-29, not hand-waved): the canonical bar is torch.testing's per-dtype default for bfloat16 = (rtol=0.016, atol=1e-5) (torch 2.12, torch/testing/_comparison.py), itself derived from the bf16 mantissa width: 7 mantissa bits -> 2^-7 ~= 7.8e-3 per ULP, and TWO independently-rounded bf16 outputs of slightly-different fp32 reductions can straddle a rounding boundary by up to 2 ULP (1.56e-2 rel at an O(1) value). The previous 1e-2 covered 1 ULP but false-failed the 2-ULP boundary case (e.g. bf16 M=128 N=512 K=512 at seed 1729: fp32 agreement was 2.3e-5 -- excellent -- yet after the cast rel hit 1.36e-2). Proven a boundary artifact, not accumulation error: scripts/archive/ds5-probes/m128_bf16_rootcause.py shows EVERY valid fp32 reduction (Kahan, naive, blocked, tree) lands at exactly 1-2 bf16-ULP vs torch after the cast. atol kept at 1e-1 (the fp8 block-scale dynamic range).

cross_backend_rtol = 0.02: raised from 1e-2 to stay >= the bf16 single-backend rtol (0.016), preserving the library.md Sec.5.4 invariant (cross-backend must not be tighter than any single-backend rtol, else a backend that passes verify would false-fail parity on the same bf16 boundary straddle). Still looser than the fp32 single-backend rtol would want, but cross-backend is intentionally loose to absorb cross-vendor accumulation-order differences (Sec.5.4); per-backend verify (fp32 rtol 1e-3) is the gate that catches real fp32 drift. out_dtype follows the point dtype. A100 (sm80) is missing_arch: no fp8 units (the Triton fp8 load does not compile — expected, not a bug).
